fixtures.name: paradigm_break_sync_file_io_async
fixtures.rationale: OOV axis: blocking open() / Path.read_text() / json.loads() tight-loop calls inside async def endpoint bodies. Corpus evidence: 0 instances of open() or .read_text() inside AsyncFunctionDef bodies at endpoint scope across docs_src/ and tests/. Async endpoints in the corpus use aiofiles or offload to threads via anyio.to_thread.run_sync() (fastapi/concurrency.py lines 32, 39). Fixture simulates a realistic document-storage API that reads and writes JSON files synchronously in every async endpoint, blocking the event loop for the full disk-operation duration.

fixtures.name: control_anyio_thread_offload
fixtures.rationale: Idiomatic FastAPI pattern for blocking I/O from async endpoints: wrap each blocking callable in functools.partial() and hand it to `await anyio.to_thread.run_sync(fn)`. Grounded in fastapi/concurrency.py lines 6, 32, 39 (`import anyio.to_thread`; `await anyio.to_thread.run_sync(cm.__exit__, ...)`) and tests/test_stream_cancellation.py (line 9: `import anyio` in async endpoint context). No blocking calls appear directly inside async def bodies — all file I/O and JSON parsing are wrapped in synchronous helper functions and dispatched to the thread pool.
